forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCAE5WVKRHTYNJYZT7F2M9K
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8680'
+project: card-comments
+title: Add comment-log display + editor and register them in the field registry (UI)
+---
+## What
+Surface the conversation log in the task inspector. The inspector (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` â†’ `EntityInspector`) renders every schema field through the metadata-driven field registry, so a new `comment-log` display/editor registered with the registry will appear automatically once the `comments` field is on the task entity (dependency task). Do NOT hardcode comment logic anywhere in inspector/EntityInspector â€” register components and let the metadata drive it (per the "UI is an interpreter of Field metadata" rule).
+
+Follow the attachment field UI precedent exactly:
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/attachment.tsx` (registration adapters)
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx`
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/attachment-editor.tsx`
+
+Files:
+1. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/comment-log-display.tsx` â€” renders the comments array as a chronological thread: each member shows the actor (resolve actor id â†’ name/avatar the same way assignees/avatar display does), the text, and a relative/absolute timestamp (reuse the existing date-display formatting). Read-only display.
+2. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/comment-log-editor.tsx` â€” a compose box to add a new comment (text input + submit) and, optionally, edit/delete affordances on existing members. Submitting dispatches the `add comment` command via `useDispatchCommand` (see how attachment-editor / other editors dispatch). Author defaults to the current actor on the backend; the editor does not need to pass actor.
+3. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/comment-log.tsx` â€” `registerDisplay("comment-log", ...)` and `registerEditor("comment-log", ...)` adapters.
+4. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/index.ts` â€” add `import "./comment-log";` (this is the central registration index; a new file MUST be imported here or it never registers).
+5. Add the `comment-log` editor/display names to the TS `FieldType`/type plumbing if the UI maintains an enum mirror (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` has a `FieldType` â€” check whether display/editor names are typed there; the Rust side treats them as free strings, mirror that).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Opening a task inspector renders the `comments` field via the `comment-log` display, showing each member's actor, text, and timestamp.
+- [ ] The editor can add a new comment; submitting dispatches `add comment` and the new member appears (reactively, via the field-change event from the events task).
+- [ ] No comment-specific branching exists in `EntityInspector`/inspector code â€” all behavior flows through the registry.
+- [ ] `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit` clean in `apps/kanban-app/ui`.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `comment-log-display.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-display.test.tsx`): given a `comments` value with two members, asserts both texts, both resolved actor labels, and timestamps render in order.
+- [ ] `comment-log-editor.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-editor.test.tsx`): typing text + submit dispatches the `add comment` command with the right args; empty submit is a no-op.
+- [ ] A browser/integration test (modeled on `board-integration.browser.test.tsx`, which already references a `comments` field shape) drives the inspector and asserts the comment thread renders from field metadata.
+- [ ] `npm test` in `apps/kanban-app/ui` â€” green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the display + editor component tests first, then implement the components and register them.
\ No newline at end of file

+Design decision: the inspector exposes **full CRUD** â€” add, edit, and delete on comment members (not add-only). Each member dispatches the matching backend op.
+
 Files:
 1. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/comment-log-display.tsx` â€” renders the comments array as a chronological thread: each member shows the actor (resolve actor id â†’ name/avatar the same way assignees/avatar display does), the text, and a relative/absolute timestamp (reuse the existing date-display formatting). +2. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/comment-log-editor.tsx` â€” a compose box to add a new comment (text input + submit), PLUS per-member edit and delete affordances on existing members. Add dispatches `add comment`; editing an existing member's text dispatches `update comment` (text-only edit â€” who/when stay fixed); deleting dispatches `delete comment`. All via `useDispatchCommand` (see how attachment-editor / other editors dispatch). Author defaults to the current/local actor on the backend; the editor does not need to pass actor.
 3. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/comment-log.tsx` â€” `registerDisplay("comment-log", ...)` and `registerEditor("comment-log", ...)` adapters.
 4. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/index.ts` â€” add `import "./comment-log";` (this is the central registration index; a new file MUST be imported here or it never registers).
 5. Add the `comment-log` editor/display names to the TS `FieldType`/type plumbing if the UI maintains an enum mirror (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` has a `FieldType` â€” check whether display/editor names are typed there; the Rust side treats them as free strings, mirror that).

@@ -9,34 +10,40 @@
 title: Add comment-log display + editor and register them in the field registry (UI)
 ---
 ## What
-Surface the conversation log in the task inspector. The inspector (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspectors-container.tsx` â†’ `EntityInspector`) renders every schema field through the metadata-driven field registry, so a new `comment-log` display/editor registered with the registry will appear automatically once the `comments` field is on the task entity (dependency task). Do NOT hardcode comment logic anywhere in inspector/EntityInspector â€” register components and let the metadata drive it (per the "UI is an interpreter of Field metadata" rule).
+Surface the conversation log in the task inspector as a metadata-driven field. The inspector renders every schema field through the field registry, so once the `comments` field is on the task entity (task 2) and registered here, it appears automatically in the new "Log" section. Do NOT hardcode comment logic in the inspector â€” register a display + editor for the `comment-log` name and let metadata drive it (per "UI is an interpreter of Field metadata").

-Follow the attachment field UI precedent exactly:
-- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/attachment.tsx` (registration adapters)
+ARCHITECTURE (confirmed): editors are **pure UI**. They emit the new field value via `onChange`/`onCommit`; `Field` persists it through `updateField` â†’ the generic `entity.update_field` command. The comment editor MUST NOT dispatch any comment-specific command and MUST NOT call `useDispatchCommand` directly â€” it just builds the new `comments` array and commits it, exactly like `attachment-editor.tsx` does (`const next = [...]; onChange?.(next)`). All server-side member id/timestamp/author logic runs in the `UpdateEntityField` comment-log normalization branch (dependency task). The editor only sends member `text` (and, for edits, the member's existing `id`); it never sends id/timestamp/author for new members.
+
+Follow the attachment field UI precedent for structure/registration:
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/attachment.tsx` (adapter + `registerDisplay`/`registerEditor`)
 - `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx`
 - `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/attachment-editor.tsx`

-Design decision: the inspector exposes **full CRUD** â€” add, edit, and delete on comment members (not add-only). Each member dispatches the matching backend op.
-
 Files:
-1. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/comment-log-display.tsx` â€” renders the comments array as a chronological thread: each member shows the actor (resolve actor id â†’ name/avatar the same way assignees/avatar display does), the text, and a relative/absolute timestamp (reuse the existing date-display formatting). Read-only display.
-2. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/comment-log-editor.tsx` â€” a compose box to add a new comment (text input + submit), PLUS per-member edit and delete affordances on existing members. Add dispatches `add comment`; editing an existing member's text dispatches `update comment` (text-only edit â€” who/when stay fixed); deleting dispatches `delete comment`. All via `useDispatchCommand` (see how attachment-editor / other editors dispatch). Author defaults to the current/local actor on the backend; the editor does not need to pass actor.
-3. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/comment-log.tsx` â€” `registerDisplay("comment-log", ...)` and `registerEditor("comment-log", ...)` adapters.
-4. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/index.ts` â€” add `import "./comment-log";` (this is the central registration index; a new file MUST be imported here or it never registers).
-5. Add the `comment-log` editor/display names to the TS `FieldType`/type plumbing if the UI maintains an enum mirror (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` has a `FieldType` â€” check whether display/editor names are typed there; the Rust side treats them as free strings, mirror that).
+1. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/comment-log-display.tsx` â€” render the comments array as a chronological thread (oldestâ†’newest). Each member shows: the **actor** resolved to name/avatar (reuse the same actor-resolution the assignees field/avatar uses â€” actor ids â†’ display via the schema/entity store; do not refetch), the **text**, and the **timestamp** (reuse the existing date/relative-time display formatting). Read-only.
+2. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/comment-log-editor.tsx` â€” PURE UI, full CRUD on the array via `onChange`/`onCommit`:
+   - **Add**: a compose box; on submit, `onChange?.([...current, { text }])` (new member has only `text` â€” server assigns id/timestamp/author). Empty text is a no-op.
+   - **Edit**: per-member inline edit of `text` only; on commit, map the array replacing that member's `text` (keep its `id`) and emit.
+   - **Delete**: per-member control; emit the array with that member filtered out.
+   - Never sends actor/timestamp/id for new members; never passes an author.
+3. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/comment-log.tsx` â€” `registerDisplay("comment-log", ...)` + `registerEditor("comment-log", ...)` adapters (FieldDisplayProps/FieldEditorProps), mirroring `attachment.tsx`.
+4. `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/registrations/index.ts` â€” add `import "./comment-log";` (a file not imported here never registers).
+5. If the UI maintains a TS `FieldType`/display-name mirror (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts`), add `comment-log` there (Rust treats display/editor names as free strings â€” mirror that).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Opening a task inspector renders the `comments` field via the `comment-log` display, showing each member's actor, text, and timestamp.
-- [ ] The editor can add a new comment; submitting dispatches `add comment` and the new member appears (reactively, via the field-change event from the events task).
-- [ ] The editor can edit an existing member's text (dispatches `update comment`) and delete a member (dispatches `delete comment`), with the thread updating reactively.
-- [ ] No comment-specific branching exists in `EntityInspector`/inspector code â€” all behavior flows through the registry.
+- [ ] Opening a task inspector renders the `comments` field via the `comment-log` display under the "Log" section, showing each member's actor (name/avatar), text, and timestamp in chronological order.
+- [ ] Add: composing + submit emits `[...current, {text}]` through `onChange`/`onCommit`; the new member appears reactively (via the field-change event) with a server-assigned author/timestamp/id.
+- [ ] Edit: changing a member's text emits the array with that member's `text` updated and `id` retained; actor/timestamp unchanged after round-trip.
+- [ ] Delete: removing a member emits the array without it; the member disappears after round-trip.
+- [ ] The editor does NOT call `useDispatchCommand` and contains no comment-specific command name; persistence flows only through `Field`/`updateField`.
+- [ ] No comment-specific branching in inspector/`EntityInspector` â€” all behavior via the registry.
 - [ ] `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit` clean in `apps/kanban-app/ui`.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `comment-log-display.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-display.test.tsx`): given a `comments` value with two members, asserts both texts, both resolved actor labels, and timestamps render in order.
-- [ ] `comment-log-editor.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-editor.test.tsx`): typing text + submit dispatches `add comment` with the right args; empty submit is a no-op; editing a member dispatches `update comment` with the member id + new text; deleting a member dispatches `delete comment` with the member id.
-- [ ] A browser/integration test (modeled on `board-integration.browser.test.tsx`, which already references a `comments` field shape) drives the inspector and asserts the comment thread renders from field metadata and that add/edit/delete round-trip.
+- [ ] `comment-log-display.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-display.test.tsx`): given a `comments` value with two members, asserts both texts, both resolved actor labels, and timestamps render in chronological order.
+- [ ] `comment-log-editor.test.tsx` (modeled on `attachment-editor.test.tsx`): submit emits `[...current, {text}]` (empty submit is a no-op); editing a member emits the array with updated text + retained id; deleting emits the array without the member. Assert the editor never calls a dispatch hook (pure UI â€” it only calls `onChange`/`onCommit`).
+- [ ] A browser/integration test (modeled on `board-integration.browser.test.tsx`, which already references a `comments` field shape) drives the inspector: add a comment, assert it renders with a resolved author and timestamp after the field-set round-trip; edit and delete round-trip.
 - [ ] `npm test` in `apps/kanban-app/ui` â€” green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` â€” write the display + editor (add/edit/delete) component tests first, then implement the components and register them.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the display + editor (add/edit/delete, pure-UI) component tests first, then implement and register.
\ No newline at end of file

-ARCHITECTURE (confirmed): editors are **pure UI**. They emit the new field value via `onChange`/`onCommit`; `Field` persists it through `updateField` â†’ the generic `entity.update_field` command. The comment editor MUST NOT dispatch any comment-specific command and MUST NOT call `useDispatchCommand` directly â€” it just builds the new `comments` array and commits it, exactly like `attachment-editor.tsx` does (`const next = [...]; onChange?.(next)`). All server-side member id/timestamp/author logic runs in the `UpdateEntityField` comment-log normalization branch (dependency task). The editor only sends member `text` (and, for edits, the member's existing `id`); it never sends id/timestamp/author for new members.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Opening a task inspector renders the `comments` field via the `comment-log` display under the "Log" section, showing each member's actor (name/avatar), text, and timestamp in chronological order.
-- [ ] Add: composing + submit emits `[...current, {text}]` through `onChange`/`onCommit`; the new member appears reactively (via the field-change event) with a server-assigned author/timestamp/id.
-- [ ] Edit: changing a member's text emits the array with that member's `text` updated and `id` retained; actor/timestamp unchanged after round-trip.
-- [ ] Delete: removing a member emits the array without it; the member disappears after round-trip.
-- [ ] The editor does NOT call `useDispatchCommand` and contains no comment-specific command name; persistence flows only through `Field`/`updateField`.
-- [ ] No comment-specific branching in inspector/`EntityInspector` â€” all behavior via the registry.
+- [ ] Opening a task inspector renders the `comments` field via the `comment-log` display, showing each member's actor, text, and timestamp.
+- [ ] The editor can add a new comment; submitting dispatches `add comment` and the new member appears (reactively, via the field-change event from the events task).
+- [ ] The editor can edit an existing member's text (dispatches `update comment`) and delete a member (dispatches `delete comment`), with the thread updating reactively.
+- [ ] No comment-specific branching exists in `EntityInspector`/inspector code â€” all behavior flows through the registry.
 - [ ] `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit` clean in `apps/kanban-app/ui`.
